message.reasoning_content:
We need to fix the validation errors. The errors indicate that fields like `source_refs` contain objects that are missing required fields (like `line`, `column`, `column_header`) when `kind` is `ecsv_cell`, or contain extra fields like `raw_value`, `start_line`, `end_line`, `context` when they should be `TextSourceRef`. The errors also suggest that the scaffold has many fields that should not be present, or that the source_refs are malformed.

Looking at the schema: `TextSourceRef` requires `kind`, `path`, `start_line`, `end_line`, `context`. It does NOT have `raw_value`. `EcsvCellSourceRef` requires `kind`, `path`, `line`, `column`, `column_header`, `raw_value`. It also has optional `component_raw_value`. So the errors indicate that some source_refs have a mixture of both types, e.g., having `start_line`, `end_line`, `context` while also having `raw_value`, and missing `line`, `column`, etc. That suggests that the scaffold I generated (which was labeled "stage 1 scaffold and roster") actually contained some filled candidate details beyond just identifiers, which was not supposed to be done yet. The Stage 1 task is to fill only extraction, method_chain, candidate_groups_considered, and the roster of candidate identifiers (no other fields). But my output included full candidate blocks with core, photometry, etc., which I must not have included because the task said "For candidates, return an EXHAUSTIVE roster of identifier stubs: one entry per object the paper treats as possibly unbound from the Milky Way, each containing ONLY the identifiers object (record_id, paper_candidate_id, gaia_source_id, all[] with source_refs). Do not include any other candidate fields yet." My output violated that. I provided full candidate objects with core, photometry, etc., which caused validation failure (since those fields would need proper source_refs). The error list mentions many fields like `core.observed_phase_space.distance` etc. which I should not have included at this stage.

Thus, I need to re-generate the JSON with only the identifiers for each candidate, keeping all other candidate fields empty/absent. The task says: "Return ONLY the JSON document." and "The roster must be complete even if there are hundreds of objects — never sample, truncate, or pick representatives". So I need to list all 25 candidates with just identifiers, no core, photometry, etc. The skeleton provided had `candidates` as an empty array. I will replace with identifier stubs.

Also, I need to ensure `extraction` status and summary are updated accordingly, and `method_chain` and `candidate_groups_considered` are filled. The provided skeleton had `extraction.status="needs_review"` and `summary=""`. I need to set appropriate status (either "candidates_found" or "no_candidates") and write a summary. Since we have 25 candidates, status = "candidates_found". I'll write a summary describing the paper and the number of included candidates.

`method_chain` I already filled in previous response, but need to ensure it's correct and within schema. It seemed okay.

`candidate_groups_considered` I had filled with two groups: HVS-prefixed and B-runaway-stars. However, in the previous response I had them both as "included" and "excluded" appropriately. But validation might not flag these because they are not within candidate records. I'll keep them.

Now, I will reconstruct the JSON with only identifiers for each candidate. The identifiers should have: record_id like "2003.12766:cand-001", etc., paper_candidate_id (the name used in paper), gaia_source_id (empty string unless known), and an `all` array of identifier records with `value` and `source_refs`. The source_refs for identifiers must be valid: they can be text refs (with kind "text", path, start_line, end_line, context) or ecsv cell refs (with kind "ecsv_cell", path, line, column, column_header, raw_value). Since we have both text and table sources for names. For the roster, it's fine to use text refs to the table files.

I need to include every HVS-numbered star (HVS1, HVS4–HVS24) and the three B stars (B485, B1085, B576). The B-runaway group I described as excluded, so I only include those three. The text says the paper treats all HVS-numbered as possibly unbound, plus B485 and B1085 as possibly unbound in Model II, and B576 (if MS) also possibly unbound. So 22 HVS + 3 B = 25 candidates.

I'll now construct the JSON accordingly. I must also ensure `extraction` and `tooling` are null or correct. The tooling is null in skeleton; I can leave as null for now but the task said to fill `extraction.tooling` with real instrument provenance, but I cannot guess those values; later stages might fill. For stage 1, I think we leave tooling null or empty. The schema allows it to be null. I'll set `tooling: null`. The extraction status should be "candidates_found". I'll generate the full JSON.

I'll also remove all candidate fields except `identifiers`. The skeleton had `core`, `photometry`, etc., but I must not include them. So each candidate record will be only `{"identifiers": {...}}`. That should satisfy the requirement "each containing ONLY the identifiers object". So no other keys.

Now produce the JSON.
